Preconditions: Admin access to Salesforce Setup and custom metadata.
Steps: Open Quickbridge_Config__mdt and QuickBooks_Config__mdt records. Verify Realm ID, active flag, start/end dates, verifier token, client ID, client secret, and redirect URI where applicable.
Test Data: QuickBooks metadata records: QuickBooks, Default, Sandbox_Config.
Expected Result: Metadata records exist and saved values match the approved QuickBooks app configuration. Secret values are not exposed to unauthorized users.

Preconditions: Valid QuickBooks sandbox company and valid connected app credentials are configured.
Steps: Start QuickBooks OAuth flow from the configuration panel. Authorize the Salesforce app in QuickBooks. Return to Salesforce callback URL.
Test Data: Valid realm ID, client ID, client secret, redirect URI.
Expected Result: Salesforce connects to QuickBooks successfully, stores realm/access context, and marks the connection usable.

Scenario: Validate OAuth failure with invalid secret
Preconditions: QuickBooks client ID is valid and client secret can be temporarily changed in test metadata.
Steps: Set an invalid Client Secret. Start OAuth flow and attempt authorization.
Expected Result: Error is shown, connection is not established, no stale successful connection state is displayed.

Preconditions: User has permission to access Field Mapping Configuration.
Steps: Navigate to Field Mapping Configuration. Select QuickBooks Online as the third-party application.
Test Data: Salesforce object list and QuickBooks object list.
Expected Result: Panel loads without console errors and displays Salesforce and QuickBooks object options.

Preconditions: Field_Mapping__mdt is deployable and mapping UI is accessible.
Steps: Map Account.Name to DisplayName, Phone to PrimaryPhone.FreeFormNumber, and Email__c to PrimaryEmailAddr.Address. Save.
Expected Result: Mapping is saved in Field_Mapping__mdt with active status and correct source/target fields.

Preconditions: Customer mapping screen is open and DisplayName is required.
Steps: Try saving a Customer mapping without DisplayName.
Expected Result: Save is blocked and a required field error is shown.

Preconditions: Field mapping UI has text, number, date, and checkbox Salesforce fields available.
Steps: Map a text Salesforce field to a numeric QuickBooks field and save.
Expected Result: System warns or blocks incompatible mapping before save.

Preconditions: Field mapping UI allows adding multiple rows.
Steps: Map two Salesforce fields to the same QuickBooks target field and save.
Expected Result: Duplicate mapping error is shown and metadata is not saved with duplicate target rows.

Scenario: Sync Salesforce Account to QB Customer
Preconditions: Valid OAuth connection and Account -> Customer mapping exist.
Steps: Create Account in Salesforce. Run sync to QuickBooks through action or batch.
Expected Result: QuickBooks Customer is created with mapped values and Salesforce stores the QuickBooks reference ID.

Scenario: Sync QB Customer to Salesforce Account
Preconditions: Valid OAuth connection and Customer -> Account mapping exist.
Steps: Create Customer in QuickBooks. Run QBCustomerToAccountSyncBatch.
Test Data: QB Customer with display name, phone, email.
Expected Result: Salesforce Account is created or updated with mapped QuickBooks values.

Scenario: Validate two-way Customer sync conflict handling
Preconditions: Same customer exists in Salesforce and QuickBooks with cross-reference IDs.
Steps: Update the same customer in Salesforce and QuickBooks. Run sync in both directions.
Test Data: Different phone/email values on each side.
Expected Result: Last-write-wins or configured conflict logic applies and no duplicate Account or Customer is created.

Scenario: Sync Salesforce Order to QB Invoice
Preconditions: Valid CustomerRef exists for related Account and Order has eligible line items.
Steps: Create Order with related Account and run QuickBooksInvoiceBatch.
Test Data: Activated Order with OrderItems, product, pricebook entry, account QB ID.
Expected Result: QuickBooks Invoice is created successfully with customer, line, amount, and reference values.

Preconditions: QuickBooksInvoiceBatch is available.
Steps: Create Order without linked Account or without QB customer reference. Run sync.
Test Data: Order with no AccountId or no Account QB ID.
Expected Result: Invoice sync fails gracefully with clear error and logs the failure.

Preconditions: Field mapping UI has object mapping configuration available.
Steps: Configure both Order -> Invoice and Invoice__c -> Invoice source mappings if the UI allows it.
Test Data: Order and Invoice__c source mappings to QB Invoice.
Expected Result: Conflict error is shown and duplicate source mapping to the same QB object is prevented.

Scenario: Sync QB Vendor to Salesforce Account
Preconditions: Valid OAuth connection and Vendor -> Account mapping exist.
Steps: Create Vendor in QuickBooks. Run QBVendorToAccountSyncBatch.
Test Data: QB Vendor with display name, phone, email, address.
Expected Result: Salesforce Account is created or updated as vendor-type account with QuickBooks Vendor ID.

Scenario: Sync Salesforce Product2 to QB Item
Preconditions: Valid OAuth connection and Product2 -> Item mapping exist.
Steps: Create Product2 and run Product2 to QuickBooks sync.
Test Data: Product2 with Name, StockKeepingUnit, UnitPrice or mapped price.
Expected Result: QuickBooks Item is created with name, SKU, price, and correct active state.

Scenario: Sync QB Item to Salesforce Product2
Preconditions: Valid OAuth connection and Item -> Product2 mapping exist.
Steps: Create Item in QuickBooks. Run QBItemToProductSyncBatch.
Expected Result: Salesforce Product2 is created or updated with mapped QuickBooks values.

Scenario: Sync QB Credit Memo to Salesforce
Preconditions: Valid mapping exists for QuickBooks CreditMemo to mapped Salesforce object.
Steps: Create CreditMemo in QuickBooks and run sync.
Test Data: CreditMemo with customer, line item, amount, transaction date.
Expected Result: Credit memo record is created in the mapped Salesforce object with line and amount data where supported.

Preconditions: An active required mapping exists and sync works.
Steps: Deactivate a required mapping and run a sync that depends on it.
Expected Result: Sync is skipped or fails with a clear mapping error and logs the issue.

Preconditions: Admin access to scheduler UI or Apex scheduler.
Steps: Schedule QuickBooksInvoiceBatch hourly.
Test Data: Hourly schedule for QuickBooks invoice sync.
Expected Result: Batch appears in Scheduled Jobs with expected cadence and next run time.

Preconditions: Eligible records exist for a QuickBooks batch.
Steps: Run QuickBooksInvoiceBatch manually.
Test Data: Set of eligible Orders or Invoices.
Expected Result: Eligible records are processed and success/failure counts are visible in logs or job status.

Preconditions: Batch input contains one valid and one invalid record.
Steps: Run QuickBooksInvoiceBatch with valid and invalid Orders.
Test Data: One valid Order, one missing CustomerRef or invalid line item.
Expected Result: Valid records process successfully; invalid records are logged without stopping the whole batch.

Preconditions: One Order has already been synced to QuickBooks and has QB invoice ID stored.
Steps: Run sync for the same Order again.
Expected Result: No duplicate QuickBooks Invoice is created; existing invoice is detected or updated.

Preconditions: A Salesforce Order is already synced to a QB Invoice.
Steps: Modify the synced Salesforce Order or lines and rerun sync.
Test Data: Changed quantity, unit price, or address.
Expected Result: Existing QuickBooks Invoice is updated using existing QB ID and SyncToken, not recreated.

Scenario: Validate deleted or invalid QB reference
Preconditions: Salesforce record stores a stale QuickBooks Customer or Invoice ID.
Steps: Delete or invalidate the QuickBooks object, then run sync.
Test Data: Stale QB Customer ID or Invoice ID.
Expected Result: Error is handled and logged with actionable message; batch does not crash.

Preconditions: Nested QuickBooks target/source fields are supported in mapping UI.
Steps: Map PrimaryEmailAddr.Address to Account.Email__c and run sync.
Expected Result: Nested QB field syncs correctly in the selected direction.

Preconditions: Required mappings exist; one optional mapping is intentionally left blank.
Steps: Run sync with optional field unmapped.
Test Data: Optional phone, website, or address field left unmapped.
Expected Result: Sync succeeds without optional field and does not write null into unrelated fields.

Preconditions: Standard user exists without metadata/configuration permissions.
Steps: Login as standard user and attempt to edit QB mappings.
Expected Result: Access is denied or save is blocked; no metadata changes are persisted.

Preconditions: Non-admin user can open part of the config UI but lacks secret access.
Steps: Open QB config as non-admin.
Test Data: QuickBooks config page with stored secret values.
Expected Result: Secret values are masked, hidden, or inaccessible; non-admin cannot retrieve client secret from UI or network payload.

Preconditions: Callout mock or network test can simulate QuickBooks timeout/unavailable API.
Steps: Run a QB sync while QuickBooks API times out or returns unavailable response.
Expected Result: Error is logged; batch does not crash; retry/manual rerun path remains possible.

Preconditions: OAuth start endpoint and callback are accessible.
Steps: Start OAuth flow, capture callback, change the state parameter, and submit callback.
Expected Result: Callback is rejected and no token or realm context is saved.

Preconditions: QuickBooks app can be configured with an invalid redirect URI in sandbox.
Steps: Set redirect URI mismatch and start OAuth.
Test Data: Redirect URI different from Salesforce callback URL.
Expected Result: QuickBooks authorization fails with a clear configuration error and Salesforce does not mark connection active.

Preconditions: Existing access token is near expiry and refresh token is valid.
Steps: Run any QuickBooks API call after access token expiry threshold.
Test Data: Expired or nearly expired access token.
Expected Result: Access token refreshes automatically and the API call succeeds without user reauthorization.

Scenario: Validate expired or revoked refresh token
Preconditions: Refresh token is revoked in QuickBooks or intentionally expired.
Steps: Run a sync that requires token refresh.
Expected Result: Sync fails with reauthorization-required message and logs the auth failure without repeated callout loops.

Preconditions: OAuth connection exists for one company.
Steps: Disconnect the app from the QuickBooks company, then run a sync.
Expected Result: Salesforce detects authorization failure and surfaces a reconnect action or clear error.

Preconditions: QuickBooks_Is_Active__c can be toggled in metadata.
Steps: Set QuickBooks_Is_Active__c to false and run sync or open QB scheduler.
Expected Result: QB sync actions are disabled or fail fast with clear inactive-configuration message.

Preconditions: QuickBooks_End_Date__c can be set to a past date.
Steps: Set QuickBooks_End_Date__c to a past date and attempt sync.
Expected Result: QuickBooks feature is unavailable and user sees product-expired message.

Preconditions: QuickBooks_Start_Date__c can be set to a future date.
Steps: Set start date in the future and attempt sync.
Expected Result: QuickBooks feature is unavailable until the start date and sync does not call QuickBooks.

Preconditions: QuickBooks metadata can be edited in a sandbox.
Steps: Clear QuickBooks Realm ID and run an API call.
Expected Result: API call is blocked with configuration error before making malformed endpoint call.

Preconditions: QuickBooks sync endpoint uses minorversion parameter.
Steps: Run invoice, credit memo, and purchase order syncs and inspect callout endpoint in debug/mock logs.
Expected Result: All relevant QuickBooks calls include expected minorversion and do not regress across object types.

Preconditions: Error_Log__c is available and logging utility is enabled.
Steps: Force a QuickBooks validation error and inspect Error_Log__c.
Test Data: Invalid invoice payload or missing CustomerRef.
Expected Result: Error log captures gateway QuickBooks, class/method, record ID where possible, status/body, and timestamp.

Preconditions: Integration_Usage_Limit.QuickBooks metadata is configured with a low limit.
Steps: Set available limit below batch size and run batch.
Test Data: Usage limit of 1 with 2 eligible records.
Expected Result: Batch is blocked or partially limited according to policy, and usage summary is not over-consumed.

Preconditions: Usage summary object is available.
Steps: Run a successful QB sync for multiple records.
Expected Result: Integration usage summary increments by successful synced record count only.

Scenario: Validate scheduler UI loads QuickBooks jobs
Preconditions: QuickBooks scheduler component is available.
Steps: Open QB scheduler page/panel.
Expected Result: Scheduler UI displays QuickBooks jobs, status, cadence, and next run without JS errors.

Preconditions: At least one QB scheduled job exists.
Steps: Pause the scheduled QB job, confirm it stops, then resume it.
Expected Result: Job status changes correctly and scheduled job behavior matches the selected state.

Preconditions: A QuickBooks scheduled job already exists.
Steps: Attempt to create the same QuickBooks schedule again.
Expected Result: System prevents duplicate scheduled jobs or replaces the existing job predictably.

Preconditions: Scheduler supports custom or advanced schedule input.
Steps: Enter invalid cron/cadence values and save.
Expected Result: Save is blocked with validation error and no scheduled job is created.

Scenario: Validate Sync To QB quick action success
Preconditions: Account page has Sync To QB quick action and valid mapping/auth.
Steps: Open Account record and click Sync To QB.
Expected Result: Action shows progress, completes successfully, and displays success toast with no duplicate action execution.

Scenario: Validate Sync To QB quick action failure
Preconditions: Account lacks a required mapped field or auth is invalid.
Steps: Open Account record and click Sync To QB.
Test Data: Account missing DisplayName source field or invalid auth.
Expected Result: Action displays failure toast with friendly message and does not leave spinner stuck.

Scenario: Validate Sync From QB quick action success
Preconditions: Account has stored QuickBooks Customer ID and valid auth.
Steps: Open Account record and click Sync From QB.
Test Data: Account linked to existing QB Customer.
Expected Result: Salesforce fields update from QuickBooks and action displays success toast.

Scenario: Validate rapid repeated quick action clicks
Preconditions: Account quick action is available.
Steps: Click Sync To QB multiple times quickly or reopen action during processing.
Expected Result: Only one sync is submitted or duplicate attempts are safely ignored; no duplicate QB customer is created.

Scenario: Validate Account address mapping to QB BillAddr
Preconditions: Address mappings are configured.
Steps: Create or update Account billing address and sync to Customer.
Expected Result: QuickBooks Customer BillAddr is populated correctly with nested address fields.

Scenario: Validate special characters in customer name
Preconditions: Valid Account -> Customer mapping exists.
Steps: Create Account with apostrophes, ampersand, slash, and non-breaking spaces if allowed, then sync.
Expected Result: QuickBooks Customer is created with valid escaped values and no JSON serialization failure.

Scenario: Validate duplicate customer display name conflict
Preconditions: QuickBooks already has a Customer with same DisplayName but no Salesforce reference.
Steps: Sync Salesforce Account with same DisplayName.
Expected Result: System handles QuickBooks duplicate-name validation by linking, warning, or logging clear conflict according to expected design.

Preconditions: Salesforce Account is linked to inactive QuickBooks Customer.
Steps: Set Customer inactive in QuickBooks and run Salesforce-to-QB update.
Expected Result: Update either reactivates only if intended or fails with a clear inactive customer error.

Preconditions: QuickBooks has an existing Vendor with same display name.
Steps: Create matching Vendor in QB or Salesforce source and run sync.
Expected Result: System avoids duplicate Account creation and logs or resolves the conflict predictably.

Preconditions: QuickBooks or Salesforce already has an Item/Product with same SKU but different ID.
Steps: Run product/item sync.
Expected Result: Sync prevents accidental merge and logs conflict or updates the intended linked record only.

Scenario: Validate inactive Product2 is not synced as sellable item
Preconditions: Product2 can be active/inactive.
Steps: Create inactive Product2 and run Product2 to QB sync.
Expected Result: Inactive product is skipped or created inactive in QuickBooks according to mapping rules.

Preconditions: Product2 price contains decimal precision.
Steps: Sync Product2 to QuickBooks Item.
Expected Result: Price is rounded or rejected according to QuickBooks currency precision rules and result is documented.

Scenario: Validate invoice line item with missing ProductRef
Preconditions: Order/Invoice line exists with product not synced to QB Item.
Steps: Run invoice sync.
Test Data: OrderItem Product2 without QB Item ID.
Expected Result: Sync fails gracefully or creates/looks up item according to design; error identifies missing ProductRef.

Preconditions: Order or Invoice has multiple line items.
Steps: Run invoice sync.
Test Data: Three line items with different quantity, unit price, and taxability.
Expected Result: QuickBooks Invoice contains all lines with correct amounts and order.

Preconditions: Tax field or item sales tax batch is configured.
Steps: Create taxable and non-taxable lines and sync invoice.
Expected Result: QuickBooks tax fields and totals match Salesforce expectation or documented tax mapping.

Preconditions: Salesforce source supports discount fields or negative line.
Steps: Create order/invoice with discount and sync.
Expected Result: QuickBooks Invoice reflects discount correctly or logs unsupported discount mapping clearly.

Preconditions: Multi-currency is enabled or currency fields are mapped.
Steps: Create source record in non-default currency and sync.
Expected Result: QuickBooks currency matches configured company support or sync is blocked with clear currency error.

Scenario: Validate invoice date and due date mapping
Preconditions: Date mappings are configured.
Steps: Create Order/Invoice with transaction date and due date, then sync.
Expected Result: QuickBooks TxnDate and DueDate are populated correctly, including timezone-safe date values.

Preconditions: Order or Invoice can be cancelled/voided in Salesforce.
Steps: Sync an already-synced record after cancelling/voiding it in Salesforce.
Expected Result: QB invoice is not duplicated; configured cancel/void behavior is applied or unsupported action is logged clearly.

Preconditions: Synced invoice has multiple lines.
Steps: Delete an OrderItem/InvoiceLine in Salesforce and rerun sync.
Expected Result: QuickBooks Invoice is updated to remove or zero the line according to expected design, without orphan line references.

Preconditions: QuickBooks invoice is changed directly after Salesforce sync.
Steps: Change QB Invoice in QuickBooks, then update Salesforce and rerun sync with stale SyncToken.
Expected Result: System retrieves latest invoice/SyncToken or logs conflict without overwriting unexpectedly.

Preconditions: Callout mock returns QuickBooks ValidationFault JSON.
Steps: Run sync that receives ValidationFault.
Expected Result: User/log message includes QuickBooks fault detail, not only generic HTTP error.

Preconditions: QuickBooks API returns HTTP 429.
Steps: Run a batch with mocked 429 response.
Test Data: HTTP 429 with retry-after header if available.
Expected Result: Batch logs rate limit, does not spin aggressively, and leaves records eligible for retry.

Scenario: Validate Salesforce Credit Memo to QB create
Preconditions: Salesforce credit memo object and lines are configured with mappings.
Steps: Create Salesforce credit memo and run QuickBooksCreditMemoBatch.
Test Data: Credit memo with customer and line item.
Expected Result: QuickBooks CreditMemo is created with correct customer, lines, amount, and stored QB ID.

Scenario: Validate QB credit memo update existing Salesforce record
Preconditions: Mapped Salesforce credit memo exists with QB ID.
Steps: Update CreditMemo in QuickBooks and run inbound sync if supported.
Expected Result: Existing Salesforce credit memo is updated, not duplicated.

Scenario: Validate Salesforce Purchase Order to QB create
Preconditions: PurchaseOrder trigger/batch is enabled and vendor/item references exist.
Steps: Create Purchase Order with line items and run QuickBooksPurchaseOrderBatch.
Test Data: Purchase Order with vendor, item, quantity, price.
Expected Result: QuickBooks PurchaseOrder is created and Salesforce stores QuickBooks PO ID.

Scenario: Validate missing VendorRef for purchase order
Preconditions: Purchase order source lacks vendor QuickBooks ID.
Steps: Run QuickBooksPurchaseOrderBatch.
Test Data: Purchase Order without vendor QB reference.
Expected Result: Sync fails gracefully and logs missing VendorRef without processing invalid payload.

Scenario: Validate purchase order update existing QB PO
Preconditions: Salesforce purchase order already synced to QuickBooks.
Steps: Modify line quantity or price and rerun sync.
Expected Result: Existing QB PurchaseOrder is updated using current SyncToken, not recreated.

Preconditions: Sales order sync batch is deployed and mapped objects exist.
Steps: Create eligible source record and run QBSalesOrderSyncBatch.
Test Data: Sales order with customer and lines.
Expected Result: Batch processes eligible record and creates/updates expected Salesforce or QuickBooks target.

Scenario: Validate QB Invoice to Salesforce Order sync
Preconditions: Valid QB Invoice exists and mapping is configured.
Steps: Create Invoice in QuickBooks and run QBInvoiceToOrderSyncBatch.
Test Data: QB Invoice with CustomerRef and line items.
Expected Result: Salesforce Order is created or updated with header and line data.

Scenario: Validate inbound invoice customer lookup failure
Preconditions: QB Invoice references a Customer not linked to Salesforce.
Steps: Run QBInvoiceToOrderSyncBatch.
Expected Result: Sync fails or creates customer first according to design; no orphan Order is created.

Preconditions: Webhook endpoint and verifier token are configured.
Steps: Send webhook payload with valid verifier/signature token.
Test Data: Valid webhook payload for customer or invoice change.
Expected Result: Webhook is accepted and routes event for processing without exposing secrets.

Preconditions: Webhook endpoint is accessible.
Steps: Send webhook payload with invalid verifier/signature token.
Expected Result: Webhook is rejected and no sync job is queued.

Preconditions: A valid webhook event has already been processed.
Steps: Resend the same webhook event payload.
Expected Result: Duplicate webhook does not create duplicate Salesforce or QuickBooks records.

Preconditions: More records exist than one batch scope size.
Steps: Run a QB batch with scope size greater than one if supported.
Expected Result: All scopes complete and per-scope failures do not lose remaining records.

Preconditions: Sandbox has large eligible data set and API limits are known.
Steps: Run bulk sync for Accounts, Products, or Invoices.
Test Data: 500 to 2000 eligible records depending on test limits.
Expected Result: Batch completes within governor/API limits or throttles gracefully with visible progress/logs.

Preconditions: Same record can be updated by trigger and manual batch.
Steps: Start manual sync while trigger-based sync is queued for same record.
Test Data: Same Order or Account in two jobs.
Expected Result: System avoids duplicate callouts or conflicting updates; final linked QB ID is stable.

Preconditions: Invoice and InvoiceLine triggers are active.
Steps: Create or update Invoice/InvoiceLine and observe triggered sync behavior.
Expected Result: QuickBooksInvoiceHandler prevents recursive processing and does not enqueue duplicate batches.

Preconditions: OrderTrigger currently has QB logic commented or disabled.
Steps: Update Order fields that would normally trigger sync.
Expected Result: No unexpected QuickBooks sync is fired when trigger logic is disabled; behavior matches release expectation.

Scenario: Validate null mapped source field handling
Preconditions: Mapping includes optional and required Salesforce source fields.
Steps: Set optional mapped field to null and required mapped field to null in separate tests, then sync.
Test Data: Null phone, null email, null required name.
Expected Result: Optional null is omitted safely; required null blocks sync with validation message.

Preconditions: Mapped Salesforce text exceeds QuickBooks field length.
Steps: Sync record with long name/email/address.
Test Data: DisplayName over QB max length, long address lines.
Expected Result: System truncates only if intended or rejects with clear error; no malformed payload is sent.

Scenario: Validate phone and email format handling
Preconditions: Phone/email fields are mapped.
Steps: Sync record with invalid phone/email formats.
Expected Result: QuickBooks accepts sanitized values or sync logs validation error clearly.

Preconditions: Date fields are mapped and user timezone differs from org timezone.
Steps: Sync transaction at date boundary near midnight.
Expected Result: QuickBooks transaction date matches Salesforce date, with no off-by-one day shift.

Preconditions: Standard user has access to Account but not all mapped fields.
Steps: Run Sync To QB as user without FLS to some mapped fields.
Expected Result: Sync respects field-level security where expected or runs only via approved system context; sensitive data is not exposed in UI.

Scenario: Validate debug logs do not expose secrets
Preconditions: Debug logging can be enabled for integration user.
Steps: Run OAuth and sync failure scenarios with debug logs enabled.
Test Data: OAuth tokens, client secret, refresh token.
Expected Result: Logs do not contain raw client secret, access token, refresh token, or Authorization header.

Scenario: Validate Named Credential or token storage access
Preconditions: Non-admin user can inspect accessible setup areas only.
Steps: Attempt to view stored token/credential artifacts as non-admin.
Expected Result: User cannot access stored credentials or token values.

Scenario: Validate CRUD restrictions on target objects
Preconditions: User lacks create/update permission for mapped Salesforce target object.
Steps: Run inbound QB sync as that user or integration context.
Expected Result: Sync uses intended integration permissions or fails cleanly with permission error; no silent partial writes.

Scenario: Validate field mapping row add/remove behavior
Preconditions: Mapping UI is open.
Steps: Add several mapping rows, delete middle row, save, and reload.
Expected Result: Only remaining rows persist and row order/required indicators remain correct.

Scenario: Validate object change clears stale fields
Preconditions: Mapping UI is open with selected source/target objects.
Steps: Select Account -> Customer fields, then change target object to Invoice.
Expected Result: Old Customer fields are cleared and cannot be saved against Invoice mapping.

Preconditions: Network can be throttled or Apex can return controlled error.
Steps: Open mapping or scheduler panel during slow/error response.
Test Data: Delayed Apex response or thrown exception.
Expected Result: Spinner, error message, and retry behavior render correctly; UI does not show stale success data.

Preconditions: Salesforce app can be opened at smaller viewport.
Steps: Open QB mapping panel on narrow viewport.
Expected Result: Controls remain usable, labels do not overlap, and save/delete controls are accessible.

Preconditions: Source package is ready for deployment.
Steps: Deploy QB-related classes, metadata, LWC, quick actions, triggers, and custom fields to clean sandbox.
Expected Result: Deployment succeeds without missing object/field/profile dependencies.

Preconditions: Valid auth, mappings, and sample data for Customer, Item, Invoice, CreditMemo, PurchaseOrder.
Steps: Run one happy-path sync for each supported QB entity after deployment.
Test Data: Account, Product2, Order/Invoice, Credit Memo, Purchase Order.
Expected Result: All supported QB paths complete successfully and leave clear IDs/status/logs for audit.
